dependencies: Dependence on third-party suppliers for data center equipment including generators and switchgear
Global supply chain challenges for data center equipment with lead-time constraints
Reliance on utility companies and third-party power providers for electricity supply
Dependence on internet service providers and telecommunications carriers for connectivity
risks: Global supply chain issues and inflation driving increased construction costs
Lack of availability and delays in delivery of data center equipment
Supply chain disruptions including potential impact from Middle East conflict affecting Red Sea shipping routes
Increased costs for building materials (steel, copper) and equipment procurement
Long lead-times to procure equipment such as generators
Risk of having to pre-buy equipment and materials before construction plans are finalized

regulatory_risks: Evolving laws and regulations in countries of operation due to recent U.S. administration change
Compliance with economic and trade sanctions (OFAC, Bureau of Industry and Security)
Compliance with anti-bribery and corruption laws
Compliance with evolving cybersecurity laws including reporting requirements (CIRCIA 2022, SEC Cybersecurity Disclosure Rule, EU NIS 2, EU DORA)
Designation as critical infrastructure or essential services leading to additional regulations
Increasing sustainability regulations requiring mandatory reporting and decarbonization
Environmental regulations on GHG emissions, carbon pricing, renewable portfolio standards
Regulations on data center growth and development in markets with power/land/water shortages
Government regulations related to internet services and data flows/data localization
Potential regulations on responsible use of AI (EU Artificial Intelligence Act)
REIT qualification requirements and tax compliance
Pillar Two global minimum tax framework (15% minimum tax)
Property tax and indirect tax compliance across multiple jurisdictions
Net neutrality regulations and FCC rule changes
Environmental regulations on power plant emissions and water usage

sanction_risks: U.S.-China trade war and restrictive executive orders against Chinese customers
Sanctions against Russia due to Ukraine conflict affecting operations and customers
Compliance with sanctions lists and screening of Russian and Ukrainian customers
Risk of expanded U.S. executive orders restricting business with Chinese companies outside the U.S.
Potential revenue impact from ceasing business with sanctioned entities
Sanctions on semiconductors and telecommunications equipment from China
Middle East conflict creating regulatory and legal complexities
